title: shadcn/ui Codebase Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the shadcn/ui monorepo — the component registry, CLI, docs site, and framework templates.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of monorepos and Turborepo

steps.title: Monorepo Overview
steps.description: shadcn/ui is a monorepo managed with pnpm workspaces and Turborepo. The three main areas are: apps/ (the documentation site), packages/ (the shadcn CLI), and templates/ (starter projects for every supported framework).

steps.title: Documentation Site (apps/v4)
steps.description: The Next.js documentation site lives in apps/v4. It contains the component examples, the registry that powers 'npx shadcn add', and all the MDX content for the docs pages.

steps.title: Component Registry
steps.description: The registry in apps/v4/registry/ defines every component, block, hook, and style that users can install. The new-york-v4/ directory contains the actual component source code that gets copied into user projects.

steps.title: The shadcn CLI
steps.description: The CLI package (packages/shadcn) powers 'npx shadcn init' and 'npx shadcn add'. It handles project detection, component installation, style application, and schema migrations. The commands/ directory maps to each CLI subcommand.

steps.title: Framework Templates
steps.description: The templates/ directory contains starter projects for every supported framework: Next.js, Vite, React Router, Astro, and TanStack Start — each with both standalone and monorepo variants. These are used by 'npx shadcn init' to scaffold new projects.

steps.title: Testing & CI
steps.description: Tests live in packages/tests/ (integration tests for the CLI) and packages/shadcn/test/ (unit tests). The project uses Vitest with workspace support. CI workflows in .github/workflows/ handle linting, testing, and releases.

steps.title: Configuration & Tooling
steps.description: The monorepo is wired together with pnpm-workspace.yaml, turbo.json (build orchestration), and shared configs at the root. The scripts/ directory handles cross-package tasks like syncing templates.
